title: Legislators
rationale: Legislation requires political negotiation, constituency representation, and judgment rooted in social context that AI cannot replace, though AI may assist with drafting and research.

title: Government Senior Officials
rationale: Policy formulation and governance require human accountability and political discretion, but document drafting, report generation, and data analysis are significantly automatable.

title: Directors and Chief Executives
rationale: Strategic leadership and organizational vision depend on human trust and contextual judgment, though AI tools increasingly assist executives with analytics, reporting, and decision support.

title: Production and Operations Managers
rationale: Operations scheduling, logistics optimization, and performance monitoring are increasingly automated by AI, though direct human oversight of workforce and machinery remains necessary.

title: Physicists and Chemists
rationale: AI assists in hypothesis generation, literature review, and data analysis, but experimental design and scientific reasoning still depend heavily on trained human expertise.

title: Computing Professionals (Software Engineers)
rationale: Code generation, debugging, and boilerplate writing are heavily assisted by LLMs, making routine programming tasks highly automatable, though system design and architecture require human judgment.

title: Architects and Civil Engineers
rationale: AI can assist with design generation and structural calculations, but site-specific engineering judgment, regulatory compliance, and ground-level inspections remain human-dependent in Nepal.

title: Life Science Professionals (Biologists)
rationale: AI accelerates genomic analysis, literature review, and data modelling, but laboratory experimentation and fieldwork in Nepal's diverse ecological zones require human presence.

title: Health Professionals (Doctors)
rationale: AI assists in diagnostic imaging and medical record analysis, but patient-facing diagnosis, surgical procedures, and clinical decisions in Nepal's resource-constrained setting remain firmly human.

title: Nursing Professionals
rationale: Nursing is deeply physical and relational — involving patient care, monitoring, and bedside empathy — which AI cannot replicate, especially across Nepal's under-resourced health facilities.

title: University and Higher Education Teachers
rationale: AI can deliver lectures, grade papers, and generate content, but nuanced mentorship, classroom management, and academic supervision in Nepal's universities still require human educators.

title: Secondary Education Teachers
rationale: AI tutoring tools can supplement instruction and grading, but classroom delivery in Nepal's diverse rural and urban schools, with variable digital access, remains human-led.

title: Business Professionals (Accountants)
rationale: Bookkeeping, financial reporting, and tax computation are highly repetitive and rule-based tasks that LLMs and accounting AI can largely automate, significantly impacting this occupation in Nepal.

title: Legal Professionals (Lawyers)
rationale: Legal research, contract drafting, and document review are highly automatable by LLMs, though court representation and judicial discretion in Nepal's legal system still require human lawyers.

title: Physical and Engineering Science Technicians
rationale: Field testing, equipment calibration, and hands-on technical work are difficult to automate, though AI supports data logging and diagnostic interpretation.

title: Computer Associate Professionals (IT Support)
rationale: Routine IT troubleshooting, helpdesk support, and system setup tasks are increasingly handled by AI chatbots and automated tools, reducing demand for lower-tier IT support roles.

title: Modern Health Associate Professionals (Lab Technicians)
rationale: Lab sample handling and specimen preparation require physical presence, though AI assists in test result interpretation and anomaly detection.

title: Finance and Sales Associate Professionals
rationale: Sales data analysis, customer outreach scripting, and financial reporting are automatable, but face-to-face client relationships remain important in Nepal's trust-based financial culture.

title: Secretaries and Office Assistants
rationale: Scheduling, email management, document formatting, and data entry are among the most automatable office tasks for LLMs, making administrative assistant roles highly exposed to AI.

title: Numerical Clerks (Cashiers / Bank Tellers)
rationale: Routine transactions, balance inquiries, and record-keeping are rapidly being automated by banking apps and AI in Nepal's expanding digital financial sector.

title: Travel Attendants and Guides
rationale: Trekking guides and tour operators in Nepal provide physical accompaniment, safety, and cultural interpretation in remote terrain where AI tools have limited real-world applicability.

title: Housekeeping and Restaurant Service Workers
rationale: Physical cleaning, table service, and hospitality work in Nepal's hotels and restaurants require manual dexterity and human interaction that AI robotics cannot yet replace affordably.

title: Shop Salespersons
rationale: Retail selling in Nepal is relationship- and trust-driven, especially in bazaars and local markets, limiting AI displacement despite growing e-commerce automation potential.

title: Street Vendors
rationale: Street vending is an entirely physical, informal, and socially embedded economic activity in Nepal, where AI tools have near-zero direct automation applicability.

title: Market-Oriented Crop Growers
rationale: Subsistence and commercial farming in Nepal relies on land management, irrigation, and seasonal labor — physical activities largely beyond current AI automation reach in the local context.

title: Skilled Livestock and Poultry Producers
rationale: Animal husbandry requires hands-on physical care, disease monitoring, and local ecological knowledge in Nepal's predominantly rural settings where AI agri-tech adoption is still nascent.

title: Skilled Forestry and Related Workers
rationale: Forest management, planting, and conservation work in Nepal requires on-ground physical activity and ecological awareness that remains largely beyond practical AI automation.

title: Miners and Stone Cutters
rationale: Extraction, cutting, and carving of stone and minerals is physical hazardous labor in Nepal that requires human presence in unstructured, remote environments.

title: Building Frame Workers (Carpenters/Masons)
rationale: Construction craft work requires manual dexterity, physical strength, and real-time adaptation to site conditions — tasks that remain highly resistant to AI automation in Nepal.

title: Metal Molders and Welders
rationale: Welding and metal molding are skilled manual trades dependent on physical precision and sensory judgment, with AI playing only peripheral roles in quality inspection.

title: Machinery Mechanics and Fitters
rationale: Mechanical repair and fitting require physical diagnostics and hands-on intervention, with AI-assisted diagnostics supporting but not replacing the mechanic in Nepal's informal repair economy.

title: Precision Workers in Metal and Jewelry
rationale: Jewelry craftsmanship in Nepal (particularly Newari metalwork) is a culturally valued artisan skill requiring manual precision and creative tradition that AI cannot authentically replicate.

title: Food Processing Workers (Butchers / Bakers)
rationale: Food processing in Nepal is predominantly manual and informal, carried out in small-scale shops and homes, making large-scale AI-driven automation economically and logistically unfeasible.

title: Wood Treaters and Cabinetmakers
rationale: Woodworking trades in Nepal involve artisan skill and hand-tool craftsmanship; AI can assist with design, but physical production remains manual.

title: Mining and Mineral Plant Operators
rationale: Plant operation involves physical machinery handling in challenging environments; AI assists in monitoring and predictive maintenance but cannot replace operators in Nepal's small-scale mining sector.

title: Metal-Processing Plant Operators
rationale: Metal processing requires physical machine operation and quality control, though AI-enabled sensors and automation are beginning to penetrate Nepal's industrial zones incrementally.

title: Chemical-Products Machine Operators
rationale: Process control for chemical production can be partially automated, though Nepal's small manufacturing base limits large-scale deployment of AI-driven automation in this sector.

title: Motor Vehicle Drivers (Bus / Truck)
rationale: Autonomous driving is technologically distant from Nepal's hilly terrain, narrow mountain roads, and unpredictable traffic patterns, meaning human drivers remain essential for the foreseeable future.

title: Agricultural and Mobile Plant Operators
rationale: Operating tractors and harvesters across Nepal's diverse terrains requires real-time human judgment, with AI-guided machinery not yet financially or practically viable at scale.

title: Street Food Vendors
rationale: Street food vending is an informal, physical, and socially interactive economic activity central to Nepal's urban economy, with essentially zero direct AI automation exposure.

title: Domestic Helpers and Cleaners
rationale: Domestic work in Nepal involves highly physical, relational, and context-dependent tasks in private households that are practically impossible to automate with current AI or robotics.

title: Building Caretakers and Watchmen
rationale: Security surveillance can be partially augmented by AI cameras, but gate-keeping, physical intervention, and on-site duties in Nepal still require human guards due to cost and technology barriers.

title: Messengers and Luggage Porters
rationale: Porterage in Nepal (especially in Himalayan trekking regions) is a physically demanding, terrain-specific role deeply embedded in the local economy that AI cannot realistically replace.

title: Agricultural and Fishery Labourers
rationale: Subsistence-level farm labor in Nepal's informal agricultural sector involves entirely physical tasks in environments where AI agri-tech infrastructure does not yet exist at scale.

title: Mining and Construction Labourers
rationale: Construction and mining labor in Nepal is unstructured, physically intensive, and dependent on human adaptability in conditions that make AI automation prohibitively difficult and expensive.

title: Manufacturing Labourers
rationale: Repetitive assembly-line tasks in Nepal's garment and carpet factories have moderate automation potential, but low labor costs and small factory scales limit practical AI/robotics adoption.

title: Transport Labourers and Freight Handlers
rationale: Loading, unloading, and manual freight handling require physical strength and real-time adaptability; Nepal's informal logistics sector has very limited exposure to warehouse automation.

title: Armed Forces Officers
rationale: Military command requires situational judgment, leadership, and ethical accountability; AI assists in intelligence analysis and logistics planning but decision-making authority remains strictly human.

title: Non-commissioned Officers and Soldiers
rationale: Frontline military and peacekeeping roles involve physical engagement and unpredictable real-world scenarios where AI tools serve only as support systems, not replacements.

title: Writers and Artists
rationale: AI can generate text and visual art, posing a moderate threat to freelance writers and graphic designers in Nepal, though culturally specific Nepali literature and traditional art retain strong human authenticity.

title: Traditional Medicine Practitioners
rationale: Ayurvedic and traditional healing in Nepal is deeply embedded in cultural trust, oral tradition, and physical treatment practices that AI cannot replicate or earn legitimacy in.

title: Primary Education Teachers (Associate)
rationale: Primary teaching involves child development, classroom management, and community engagement; while AI tutoring apps exist, rural Nepal's low digital connectivity limits their practical impact.
